channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Size of slim Republican House majority hangs on 5 uncalled races
item.description: There are still eight uncalled House races that will determine the size of the Republican majority in Congress after the 2024 elections.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon bracing for sweeping changes after Trump nominates Pete Hegseth for secretary
item.description: The Pentagon is bracing for sweeping policy changes under the incoming Trump administration, and some high-level officers could find their careers on the chopping block.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gabbard's sympathetic views toward Russia cause alarm as Trump's pick to lead intelligence services
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Tulsi Gabbard, President-elect Donald Trump's choice to lead the U.S. intelligence services, in 2022 endorsed one of Russia's main justifications for invading Ukraine: the existence of dozens of U.S.-funded biolabs working on some of the world's nastiest pathogens.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, ELLEN KNICKMEYER and EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump is creating team of loyalists after conflicts with Cabinet members in 1st term
item.description: In his second term, President-elect Donald Trump is surrounding himself with people who can amplify his message and agree with how he wants to remake the executive branch.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Trump-DeSantis complicated relationship could impact Senate pick: political expert
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis has a big decision to make if Sen. Marco Rubio is confirmed as the next Secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hubbard County election judge faces felony charges for allegedly allowing unregistered voters to cast ballots
item.description: Prosecutors in Hubbard County say Timothy Michael Scouton — who was head judge at a precinct in rural Badoura Township — allowed 11 people to vote even though they had not registered.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Ryan Walters filmed himself praying for Trump, made students watch it
item.description: Walters also announced Thursday the purchase of more than 500 Bibles for use in advanced placement government classes.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Trump picks former Georgia congressman for VA secretary
item.description: President-elect Trump has announced former Rep. Doug Collins (R-Ga.), a chaplain in the Air Force Reserve, as his pick for the next Secretary of Veterans Affairs.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Appeals court allows Indiana gender-affirming care ban to remain in effect
item.description: Indiana’s Republican Gov. Eric Holcomb signed the law, Senate Bill 480, last spring, one day after he called the measure “clear as mud.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Satire slinger The Onion buys Alex Jones' Infowars at auction with help from Sandy Hook families
item.description: The satirical news publication The Onion won the bidding for Alex Jones’ Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, backed by families of Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting victims whom Jones owes more than $1 billion in defamation judgments for calling the massacre a hoax, the families announced Thursday
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Jacky Rosen looks forward to 2nd term with focus on economy; Nevada senator strongly opposes mass deportation
item.description: Democratic Nevada Sen. Jacky Rosen plans to focus on the economy and kitchen-table issues after voters re-elected her to a second term in Congress, she told 8 News Now.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: North Las Vegas city councilwoman wins re-election by 9 votes
item.description: North Las Vegas City Councilwoman Ruth Garcia-Anderson won re-election by 9 votes -- a difference of 0.04%, according to unofficial election results updated Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What can Florida expect from the next legislative session
item.description: Florida legislature meets for an organization session 14 days after the general election to swear in new members and speak on what the year ahead could look like at the capitol.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Thousands of votes never counted across Nevada due to signature issues
item.description: More than 9,000 mail-in ballots across Nevada were not counted because voters did not verify their signatures before the curing deadline, according to data the 8 News Now Investigators reviewed.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump picks Marco Rubio as his Secretary of State
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump named Sen. Marco Rubio of Florida as his nominee for secretary of state on Wednesday, setting up a onetime critic who evolved into one of the president-elect’s fiercest defenders to become the nation’s top diplomat.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and JILL COLVIN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden hosts Trump at White House in post-election tradition
item.description: President-elect Trump and President Biden are readying for what promises to be a highly awkward meeting at the White House on Wednesday as Biden welcomes his successor — a man he labeled a danger to the country — to his residence.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Trump asks Rep. Mike Waltz, China hawk, to be his national security adviser
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has asked U.S. Rep. Michael Waltz, a retired Army National Guard officer and war veteran, to be his national security adviser, a person familiar with the matter said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Americans stock up on emergency birth control, abortion pills ahead of second Trump presidency
item.description: Medication abortion providers reported a surge in requests for and purchases of the drugs in the hours after President-elect Trump was reelected.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5
